narrative: FY2022-23 is weak as a benchmark year for the related-party metrics because the four RPT percentage metrics are missing across Tata, JSW, SAIL, and Jindal, so all are ranked last under the rule. The more meaningful FY2023 comparison is therefore on sustainability capex and R&D orientation. Tata leads capex at 23% on a consolidated basis and ties with JSW at 100% on R&D alignment, while SAIL reports 0% capex and 9.02% R&D, and Jindal reports only 0.02% on both sustainability capex and R&D. Tata’s FY2023 boundary is materially different because it is consolidated and includes Indian plus international operations, so its leadership cannot be read as pure standalone India superiority. Still, from a management perspective, Tata already had a broader sustainability investment frame than SAIL and Jindal. Jindal’s low FY2023 base also reflects the early stage of its later FY2024/FY2025 sustainability investment ramp.
